summary: NEW FEATURES VERIFIED (backend + frontend): (1) STREAMING AI CHAT via SSE at POST /api/ai/chat/stream and (2) APScheduler daily reminder job + admin-only manual batch POST /api/push/run-batch. Backend: 54/54 pytest tests pass sequentially (added 7 new tests — TestAIChatStream(3) + TestSchedulerBatch(4) — on top of the 47 regression tests from iteration_4). Streaming endpoint returns Content-Type: text/event-stream, emits multiple `data: {"delta":...}` chunks and terminates with `data: {"done":true}`; both the user message and the assistant message are persisted (history grows by exactly 2); conversation memory across two streaming turns verified — the model correctly recalled a pet name 'Fulmine' introduced in turn 1 when asked in turn 2. Batch: /push/run-batch returns 401 anonymous, 403 with detail 'Solo admin' for a freshly-registered non-admin, and {total:int} (int, ≥0) for admin@pawcare.it/admin123. Regression: /push/check-reminders still returns {sent:int} per-user. Scheduler startup log line 'Reminder scheduler started (daily 08:00 UTC)' confirmed in /var/log/supervisor/backend.*.log, plus 'Reminder batch complete: 0 notifications sent across 0 users' from the manual admin trigger. Frontend E2E on the public URL (headless Chromium 1440x900) logged in as admin, navigated to /assistente, sent 'Ciao, in una frase: cosa mangia un gatto?', and observed: (a) typing indicator (3 bouncing dots) visible at t≈0-0.4s inside the empty assistant bubble [data-testid=message-assistant]; (b) progressive token accumulation 0→1→65→116 chars over ~2s (3 growth transitions); (c) send-button disabled while streaming; (d) chat-input and chat-send-button fully functional. No console errors, no 4xx/5xx during the flow. Regression: /dashboard still loads cleanly. Full regression from iteration_4 (auth, pets CRUD, records, guides, weights, push subscribe/unsubscribe/status/test, PWA manifest+sw, AI advice, AI chat memory) all still green.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py ai_chat_stream: the user message is persisted BEFORE the LLM stream begins, but the assistant message is persisted inside the generator only after the stream completes. If the client disconnects mid-stream (e.g. network drop), the assistant record still gets written with the partial `full` text — good. However if the LLM raises before any TextDelta, `full` is empty and an empty-content assistant row is inserted; consider skipping the insert when full==''.

server.py ai_chat_stream: initial_messages is built BEFORE the current user message is inserted, which is correct — but the same message is ALSO passed via UserMessage(text=message_text) to chat.send_message. Depending on emergentintegrations semantics, the user's message may be duplicated in the model's context. Verify against LlmChat docs; low risk since chat memory tests still pass.

server.py push_run_batch: relies on user.get('role')=='admin' but role is only set by seed_admin when the admin row is created for the first time. If admin was created without a role (older environments) the batch will 403 for the admin too. Consider a startup migration that back-fills role='admin' on the admin email. (Not observed in this run — admin has role.)
server.py send_all_reminders: runs sequentially per user; for large user bases consider asyncio.gather with a semaphore to parallelize pushes.
server.py: @app.on_event still deprecated (already flagged iteration_4) — migrate to lifespan.
server.py: `requests` sync HTTP inside async /auth/google/session (already flagged iteration_4).

pytest.ini forces `-n 2 --dist loadscope`; module-scoped `new_user_session` + `admin_session` fixtures work correctly under serial `-n 0` (as run here) but can flake under `-n 2` if TestSchedulerBatch and TestPets run in different workers. Serial run (`-n 0`) is the reliable option.
backend_test.py `import json as _json` was placed at line 594 (inside file, after other imports) — cosmetic; move to top-of-file with the other imports.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestAIChatStream with 3 tests + TestSchedulerBatch with 4 tests; total now 54)
test_credentials: admin@pawcare.it / admin123 (verified working, role=admin). Non-admin test users are ephemeral test_stream_*@example.com / test_*@example.com created per-test-class with password 'Pass1234!'.
seed_data_creation: TestAIChatStream registers a fresh 'test_stream_*@example.com' user for a clean chat history. TestSchedulerBatch reuses module-scoped `new_user_session` (a fresh test_*@example.com throwaway) for the 403 assertion, and the pre-seeded admin for the 200 case. No persistent test data added.
rca of the issue: N/A — both new features work end-to-end as specified. Streaming SSE contract matches the review request (text/event-stream, `delta` chunks, `done` terminator, both messages persisted, memory preserved). Manual batch enforces admin-only with the exact Italian detail 'Solo admin'. Scheduler is registered and starts with the documented log line.
